text: Object camouflaging occurs when an object blends its texture with its surrounding context, rendering it difficult to detect based on visual appearance alone. Existing camouflaged object detection (COD) methods primarily rely on either location information or appearance cues, which are not sufficient to fully reveal the presence of camouflaged objects. Therefore, this paper proposes a deep gradient network (DGNet) based on object gradient supervision for the detection of camouflaged objects. To facilitate the detection of camouflaged objects, DGNet is formulated as a deep framework consisting of two closely related branches named context and texture encoder. The gradient-induced transition from context features to texture features between the two encoders acts as a soft grouping cue for camouflaged objects. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed DGNet outperforms all the existing COD models in terms of detection performance. Meanwhile, our efficient version of DGNet, named DGNet-S, detects camouflaged objects in real-time with only 6.82% parameters, which is comparable to the state-of-the-art model JCSOD-CVPR21. We also demonstrate the applications of DGNet in polyp segmentation, defect detection, and transparent object segmentation, and the code of our proposed DGNet will be available at https://github.com/GewelsJI/DGNet.
explanation: The passage is highly polished and follows a standard scientific abstract structure with plausible but somewhat generic claims. While it could be human-written, its uniform style and lack of granular experimental specifics are also characteristic of AI-generated text.
